description: Having the qualities of earth - grounded, fertile, solid, nurturing. It describes things that partake of earth's nature: stability with life-giving potential.
rationale: The word 'nomei' builds on 'noma' (earth) with a flowing extension. The progression 'no-me-i' moves from grounding ('no') through embodiment ('me') to a quality marker ('i'). The vowel hiatus 'e-i' creates an adjectival feeling, transforming the noun into a descriptor.
